text.title: competitive-intelligence Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-intelligence as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Skipping the fact-checker to save tokens on the synthesis pass.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single hallucinated feature claim in a battlecard that contradicts the competitor's actual product will be caught by a prospect, not the agent.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated synthesis: LLMs invent competitor features when scraped page is paywalled or JS-rendered and returns empty body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require source citation per claim. Reject unsourced assertions.

failure-mode.description: Pricing pages hidden behind "Contact Sales"—daily monitors generate false silence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-check G2/Capterra and customer reviews instead for pricing signal.

failure-mode.description: Translation drift in non-English markets—Crayon/Klue auto-translate loses nuance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use Valona for multilingual CI or manual review per region.

failure-mode.description: Regulatory limits: scraping LinkedIn at scale violates ToS; LinkedIn API is gated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use Phantombuster/Apify with proxy rotation and honor robots.txt or pay for Crunchbase/AlphaSense API.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship bias in win-rate claims (16% → 45%)—usually conflates CI rollout with sales enablement and pricing changes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Audit methodology before accepting vendor case study claims. Tie CI improvements to quarterly lost-deal analysis.
